name: PEFT (Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning)

auth.notes: HF_TOKEN for Hub operations; no auth for local use

performance.notes: LoRA reduces trainable parameters by 90%+ vs full fine-tuning; significant VRAM savings

agent_readiness.notes: Parameter-efficient fine-tuning methods for large models. Methods: LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation), QLoRA (quantized LoRA), LoHa, LoKr, AdaLoRA, Prefix Tuning, P-Tuning, Prompt Tuning, IA3. LoRA adds trainable rank decomposition matrices to frozen base model — 90%+ reduction in trainable parameters. Works with transformers and diffusers. PeftModel wraps base model. Merge adapters back into base for deployment. Multi-adapter support. Standard for efficient LLM customization without full fine-tuning.

gotchas: LoRA configuration (rank, alpha, target modules) requires domain knowledge
Adapter merging for deployment adds complexity
QLoRA (4-bit) can affect training stability vs full precision
Different PEFT methods suit different tasks — no universal best
best_when: Fine-tuning LLMs on consumer GPUs (16-24GB VRAM)
Domain adaptation of LLMs with limited data
Image generation model adaptation (Stable Diffusion LoRAs)
avoid_when: Full fine-tuning is feasible and accuracy is critical
summary: PEFT provides parameter-efficient fine-tuning methods for large models — primarily LoRA and QLoRA. Reduces trainable parameters by 90%+ while achieving near full fine-tuning performance. Works with HuggingFace transformers and diffusers. Supports LoRA, QLoRA, AdaLoRA, Prefix Tuning, and more. Standard approach for fine-tuning LLMs on consumer GPUs. Adapters can be merged back into base model for zero-overhead inference.
